---
title: "FRED Tech Employment (BLS via Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)"
description: "Annual national employment counts for tech occupations (SOC 15-XXXX, Computer and Mathematical Occupations), sourced from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Current Population Survey and republished through the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED data service."
author: "Mapping & Data Analytics Team"
date: "2026-05-08"
categories: ["Dataset", "Labor Market", "Technology"]
tags: ["FRED", "BLS", "Tech Employment", "SOC", "National", "Annual"]
dataset:
  name: "FRED Tech Employment (BLS Series LEU0254476900A)"
  source: "Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis"
  sourceUrl: "https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/LEU0254476900A"
  accessMethod: "FRED CSV Download"
  updateFrequency: "Annual"
  geographicLevel: "National"
  dataFormat: ["CSV"]
  featured: false
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

## Overview

The Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED data service republishes select labor market indicators sourced from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. CORI uses FRED series **LEU0254476900A** — annual national employment in Computer and Mathematical Occupations (SOC major group 15-0000) — to chart tech-employment growth from 2007 (pre-recession baseline) through 2025.

The underlying observations originate from BLS but are accessed via FRED because FRED's flat CSV endpoints provide a stable, scriptable download path that does not require BLS API authentication.

::: callout-note
## Why FRED is essential for tech-employment time series

- **Stable single-series CSV endpoint** — pulls return uniform schema regardless of revision date.
- **Long historical run** — annual observations available back to 2000 for major occupational groups.
- **Direct attribution to BLS** — series IDs are documented and citation-stable.
- **Recession bar overlays** — FRED graph configuration can be embedded in URLs for reproducible visualizations.
:::

## Coverage

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Geographic Coverage**

- National (United States only)
- No state, MSA, or county breakdowns
- Source occupational data is national-only by design
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Temporal Coverage**

- Annual observations, 2000–present
- 2007 baseline used for momentum charts
- Updated annually as BLS releases new estimates
:::
:::

## Key Variables

The FRED endpoint returns a two-column CSV: `DATE` and `LEU0254476900A` (employment count, in thousands). The series is defined as:

- **Universe**: Employed persons aged 16 and over in the United States
- **Occupational scope**: SOC major group 15-0000 (Computer and Mathematical Occupations) — software developers, data analysts, computer programmers, computer support specialists, etc.
- **Estimation**: Annual averages computed from monthly Current Population Survey (CPS) data
- **Units**: Thousands of persons

CORI's tech-employment time series uses this single series and normalizes to a 2007 baseline.

## Access

```r
# Direct CSV pull from FRED
data <- data.table::fread(
  "https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=LEU0254476900A"
)
```

The full FRED graph URL with formatting parameters is documented in `R/viz/presentation/2007_2025_tech_jobs_viz.R` and `R/viz/presentation/create_line_chart_fred_2000_2025_tech_jobs_viz.R` in the `rural-economic-outlook` project. No authentication required.

Series page: <https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/LEU0254476900A>
